creator: grahamperrin@freebsd.org
text:
Created attachment 232183
Screenshot: example A

In some situations: using the scroll bar of a sidebar to scroll to foot of the _bar_ fails to reveal the _content_ at the foot of the sidebar. 

Example A
=========

At <https://docs.freebsd.org/en/books/handbook/> (short HTML) when a person points to most links in the sidebar, the partially obscured link to 'FreeBSD Glossary' can become completely obscured (by, for example, the URL hint that's integral to Firefox). 

The link to 'Colophon' is completely invisible. 

After scrolling one bar (for the sidebar) to its fullest extent, it's necessary to _also_ scroll a second bar (farthest from the sidebar) to reveal the sidebar content that's above the standard page footer.
